title: Pungent Silky Uruguayan Horseradish Gratin
description: A pungent, silky, lacto-vegetarian-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring horseradish and kombu-cold-extract, perfect for a sick day.
instructions: Peel the horseradish roots and grate them using a box grater.
Soak the kombu in cold water for 30 minutes, then extract the liquid and reserve.
In a saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to combine. Cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the vegetable broth, whisking constantly. Bring to a simmer.
Add the milk and grated cheese. Whisk until smooth.
Remove from heat and stir in the kombu extract and grated horseradish.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Top with additional grated cheese if desired.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the gratin is set.
Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.
